title: Customer Persona Development
description: Build 3 detailed customer personas for a real or fictional brand, based on research and interviews. You'll learn to define who your audience really is beyond basic demographics.
whyItMatters: Every marketing decision starts with 'who is this for?' Personas transform vague audience descriptions into vivid, actionable profiles that guide copy, design, channel selection, and offer creation.

steps.title: Choose your subject
steps.description: Pick a brand or business to create personas for. Could be a local café, an online store you like, or a fictional startup idea.

steps.title: Conduct research interviews
steps.description: Talk to 3-5 people who are or could be customers of this brand. Ask open-ended questions: What problem were you solving? How did you find the brand? What nearly stopped you from buying?
steps.tips: Don't ask leading questions. Let people tell their story. The insights come from the unexpected details.

steps.title: Identify patterns
steps.description: Review your interview notes. What patterns emerge? Group similar people together — these groups become your persona segments.

steps.title: Build 3 personas
steps.description: For each persona, create a detailed profile: Name, age, job, goals, frustrations, media habits, decision triggers, preferred channels, objections to buying.

steps.title: Add a day-in-the-life narrative
steps.description: Write a short paragraph describing a typical day for each persona. This brings them to life and helps you understand their context.

steps.title: Map personas to marketing implications
steps.description: For each persona, write: What message resonates with them? Which channels reach them? What offer would convert them?

deliverables: One recommendation on which persona to prioritise
tools: Canva or Figma (for visual persona cards)

rubric.description: Personas are based on real conversations, not assumptions.
Each persona includes goals, frustrations, behaviours, and media habits — not just demographics.
Each persona has clear marketing implications — channel, message, and offer recommendations.
The 3 personas are clearly distinct from each other, representing different segments.
